factors.summary: Demand is accelerating, driven by elevated e-commerce activity and strong volume growth from SMBs.

evidence.text: In the first quarter, overall growth was driven by business-to-consumer demand that resulted from elevated e-commerce activity due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. We continued to experience strong volume growth from small- and medium-sized businesses ('SMBs'), largely driven by our efforts to improve time-in-transit in our U.S. ground network and the ease of accessing our services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Demand is accelerating due to elevated e-commerce activity and strong SMB growth.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with evidence of base rate increases and capacity surcharges.

evidence.text: Revenue and revenue per piece increased due to a favorable shift in customer mix, with a significant increase in SMB volume, base rate increases and capacity surcharges.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Successfully raising prices through base rate increases and capacity surcharges.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in premium products.

evidence.text: Revenue per piece increases for our Next Day Air and Deferred products were driven by base rates increases and shifts in customer and product mix, partially offset by a decrease in average billable weight per piece.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Shifting towards higher-margin segments, particularly in premium products.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is strong, with double-digit revenue per piece growth across segments.

evidence.text: Revenue increased in all segments, with double digit revenue per piece growth in both our U.S. Domestic Package and International Package segments.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong organic growth with double-digit revenue per piece growth across segments.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in residential deliveries, driven by e-commerce growth.

evidence.text: Average daily package volume increased, driven by growth in business-to-consumer shipping. Business-to-consumer shipments represented approximately 60% of total average daily volume in the first quarter, compared to approximately 55% in the first quarter of 2020.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing, particularly in residential deliveries, driven by e-commerce growth.

factors.summary: E-commerce and healthcare end markets are growing fastest, with strong demand in premium and non-premium products.

evidence.text: The International Package segment experienced volume and revenue growth across all regions, driven by growth from both large customers and SMBs. Revenue and revenue per piece increased due to growth in premium and non-premium products, base rate increases and capacity surcharges.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: E-commerce and healthcare end markets are growing fastest with strong demand.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly fuel and transportation, are rising, but the company is offsetting these through pricing.

evidence.text: Fuel Surcharges: We apply a fuel surcharge to domestic air and ground services that is adjusted weekly. The air fuel surcharge is based on the U.S. Department of Energy’s ('DOE') Gulf Coast spot price for a gallon of kerosene-type jet fuel, while the ground fuel surcharge is based on the DOE’s On-Highway Diesel Fuel price.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are rising but being offset by pricing actions.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher headcount, wage inflation, and benefit expenses, creating margin pressure.

evidence.text: Employee compensation and benefit costs increased $975 million, largely resulting from: residential volume growth and an increase in average daily direct union labor hours of 13.3%; union pay rate increases that were partially offset by productivity improvements; and management payroll increases as a result of salary increases, growth in the overall size of the workforce and increases in incentive compensation and commission payments.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing, creating margin pressure.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to higher revenue and cost management.

evidence.text: Operating margin expanded in all segments. Operating profit increased and operating margin expanded in all segments.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Gross and operating margins are expanding due to higher revenue and cost management.

factors.summary: Cost reduction initiatives are ongoing, but specific savings figures are not detailed in the text.

evidence.text: Transformation strategy costs include a valuation allowance against assets held for sale of $66 million. For additional information regarding assets held for sale, see note 6 to the unaudited, consolidated financial statements included within this report.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Cost reduction initiatives are ongoing, but specific savings figures are not detailed.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through investments in time-in-transit improvements and digital access.

evidence.text: We are continuing to expand our weekend operations and enhance our digital access program. The Customer First component of our strategy focuses on, among other things, enhancing the capabilities that we believe our customers value the most: speed and ease of access to our services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity is improving through investments in time-in-transit improvements and digital access.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share through improved service and e-commerce growth.

evidence.text: Business-to-consumer volume grew by approximately 24% year over year, driven by continued e-commerce growth. Volume growth came from both SMBs and large customers.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is gaining market share through improved service and e-commerce growth.

factors.summary: Competitive dynamics are intense, but the company is differentiating through service improvements.

evidence.text: Our investments to improve time-in-transit within our U.S. ground network also increased expense for the quarter.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Competitive dynamics are intense, but the company is differentiating through service improvements.

factors.summary: Management is confident in future performance, with expectations for continued growth.

evidence.text: We continue to expect the higher level of residential deliveries to persist. In our Supply Chain & Freight segment, market demand was elevated in nearly all business units, led by Forwarding and our healthcare activities.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident in future performance, with expectations for continued growth.

factors.summary: Management is confident, as evidenced by investments in growth initiatives and infrastructure.

evidence.text: We continue to make progress implementing our Customer First, People Led, Innovation Driven strategy, which focuses on transforming nearly every aspect of our business, improving our financial performance, providing the best customer experience and benefiting our shareowners.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Management is confident, as evidenced by investments in growth initiatives and infrastructure.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include foreign exchange fluctuations and fuel cost increases, but these are manageable.

evidence.text: Currency-neutral revenue, revenue per piece and operating profit information allows users of our financial statements to understand growth trends in our products and results. We evaluate the performance of our International Package and Supply Chain & Freight segments on this currency-neutral basis.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: Near-term risks include foreign exchange fluctuations and fuel cost increases, but these are manageable.

factors.summary: Long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on customer-first transformation and innovation.

evidence.text: We continue to make progress implementing our Customer First, People Led, Innovation Driven strategy, which focuses on transforming nearly every aspect of our business, improving our financial performance, providing the best customer experience and benefiting our shareowners.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Clear strategic focus on customer-first transformation and innovation with evidence of progress.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include fuel prices and foreign exchange movements.

evidence.text: Fuel Surcharges: We apply a fuel surcharge to domestic air and ground services that is adjusted weekly. The air fuel surcharge is based on the U.S. Department of Energy’s ('DOE') Gulf Coast spot price for a gallon of kerosene-type jet fuel, while the ground fuel surcharge is based on the DOE’s On-Highway Diesel Fuel price.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Fuel prices and foreign exchange movements pose risks to the business.

factors.summary: Digital automation is being adopted, particularly through enhancing digital access programs.

evidence.text: As a result, we are continuing to expand our weekend operations and enhance our digital access program.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Enhancing digital access programs indicates positive investment in efficiency and customer access.

factors.summary: Supply chain health is improving through efficiency gains and capacity investments.

evidence.text: Our investments to improve time-in-transit within our U.S. ground network also increased expense for the quarter.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement through efficiency gains and capacity investments, despite increased expenses.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing to meet demand, particularly in residential deliveries.

evidence.text: Average daily package volume increased across all products in the first quarter, despite the impact of one less operating day, with growth strongest in residential ground services.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capacity utilization to meet demand, especially in residential deliveries, indicates growth.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are favorable, with recovery acting as a tailwind.

evidence.text: The overall economic environment is a tailwind for the company's business, with recovery and strong GDP growth supporting demand.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Favorable economic conditions and recovery act as a tailwind for business growth.

factors.summary: Foreign exchange movements are a risk but also provide benefits in certain regions.

evidence.text: Currency-neutral revenue, revenue per piece and operating profit information allows users of our financial statements to understand growth trends in our products and results. We evaluate the performance of our International Package and Supply Chain & Freight segments on this currency-neutral basis.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Foreign exchange movements present both risks and benefits, with evidence of strategic evaluation.

factors.summary: Capital expenditure is increasing to support growth and efficiency.

evidence.text: We continue to make progress implementing our Customer First, People Led, Innovation Driven strategy, which focuses on transforming nearly every aspect of our business, improving our financial performance, providing the best customer experience and benefiting our shareowners.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Increasing capital expenditure to support growth and efficiency is a positive indicator.
